title: Future of the transit system in America - Request

transcript.text: Another request that I'll do from Jeremy Gonzalez.
He goes, Kyle, what is the future of the transit system of America?
Another offbeat question.
Do you think transportation directly affects your state of mind and subconsciousness?
Okay, so what's the future of the transit system in America?
In Amérique?
I think Amérique is a very, I think we have the best transit system in the world
Because we don't have the mass transit.
Mass transit is way better for the environment,
But I'm just kind of waiting until, optimistic,
That in the future we'll have battery-powered, electric-powered cars, right?
And so once we have that,
Then that'll kind of make all that mass transit obsolete, sort of.
You know, like if you're in the middle, the east coast,
And you have to commute a lot,
Then yeah, you'll do the mass transit
Because you get to read, you know, the newspaper
And listen to your audio MP3 or whatever
And just zone out while the thing zooms by.
But most people just like to jump in their cars and go somewhere, you know,
Like if it's camping or go to another state.
They like to drive themselves, and it's always going to be like that.
And so the future is going to be electric cars and big superhighways.
They're making a superhighway in Texas, I heard,
Like with speed limits that are really high.
They're making one that's going to be 85 miles an hour in Texas,
And so that's going to be all over.
It's going to be 100 miles an hour, man,
Once they make really fast, safe, fast cars that can even drive themselves.
And you just get in your own car, you know,
Your little minivan with your family with the privacy,
And then you can turn off and stop whenever you want, you know.
But, you know, when we have a really developed country in the future,
We will have mass transit that goes all the way across.
We'll have the bullet trains that go across America at 150 miles an hour.
When will that be?
Probably not for a while.
We need to stop spending money on the 20% of our budget on defense.
And, yeah, another offbeat question.
Do you think transportation directly affects your state of mind and subconscious?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
It's movement.
Anything that has movement charges you.
The magnetic field of the Earth is charged by the rotation of the Earth.
If the Earth stops rotating,
A lot of people think that our gravitational field will go away
And will float out into outer space.
But we do know if the Earth starts rotating,
Stops rotating,
We'll lose our magnetic field
And we won't be able to, you know,
Communicate with each other with radio signals and stuff.
That's how electric generators work.
They spin around really fast
And then they create the energy.
So, and torsion fields,
Hyper-dimensional energy from stuff spinning around.
So when you're doing, you know, exercise,
Get your body in shape.
You know, exercising your brain,
Get your brain in shape.
Same thing with transportation.
Moving and changing scenery is,
Oh my God, it's so amazing.
Like when I lived in my van,
And it was the most amazing experience of my life
And I got to do it.
As soon as I pay my friend off,
Probably at the end of next summer,
I'll live in my van again
And I'll do the rest of America
Until something fucking catastrophic happens,
Like my engine explodes again.
But, yeah, just like living,
See, I had a membership at a Gold's Gym
And I think I might want to get a membership
At a Gold's Gym again
Just because they make you move every two weeks.
And I like having to move every two weeks.
Maybe, maybe I'll do that.
But some, not all the Gold's Gyms participate
And it's kind of a pain,
But, and some of them don't let you use your computer
In the Gold's Gym and all this crap.
But anyway, I was moving every two weeks.
I was changing neighborhoods within cities and cities
And, like, I was a native of a different neighborhood
Every two weeks.
I was a native of the place.
I go into the Starbucks
And become familiar with the baristas there
And see the different customers there
And you get to capture the vibe of the place,
Just looking at the houses, driving through,
Going into the library at all the different places
And you're in it, you're, you're, you live there, you know?
It's like, it's like a timeless,
A timeless time, you know?
Like, somebody can, somebody can be friends
With somebody for a week
And they're really good friends
And then you look back in your life
And, you know, you're like,
Oh, that friend who I was with for a week
And then that friend who wasn't,
So it was close to me or whatever,
Who I was friends with for five years.
I mean, you look back on them
And they're both your friend.
It doesn't matter for how long.
And it's the same thing with living someplace
Or, you know, traveling.
And you're bouncing around
And if you're, if you go in one place
And you go to another place,
You remember the place,
You soak up as much as you're going to soak up about it
And then you move on
And, and, you know, staying there longer
Wouldn't do you any good.
So it's kind of like living in lots of different places
And traveling all over the place all the time.
You're, you're actually lengthening your life.
You're making your life longer
Because you're having more experiences in more places
And it makes, makes, uh, the time go by.
Like a lot happens in a lot, little amount of time.
But then of course there's,
There's the other flip side to that
In that you're having a lot of time flies
When you're having fun.
So years can go by like that.
And then you're like, oh my God, I'm 40 years old.
What did I do with my life?
Oh, I went here and I went there.
Oh my God, I did all that.
Ah, but, uh, yeah, that's kind of one thing
About having kids.
Cause like that can make you feel
Like you've accomplished something.
But, but, um, but, uh, but yeah,
Traveling around is an amazing experience.
Um, and it just fills you with energy.

It creates your soul.
It creates energy for your soul and motivation.
And, uh, you know, it just fills your heart with joy

That you connect to your car.
And, uh, those people who were like,

Don't listen to Kyle.
Those people are fucking like drug addicts or something.

Uh, yeah.
So, uh, yeah, you should definitely do it.

That's like riding, you know,
